back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Take Aaron and his sons with him, the garments, the anointing oil, the bull of the sin offering, the two rams, and the basket of unleavened bread,
And assemble the whole congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And Moses did as the Lord commanded him, and the congregation gathered at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And Moses said to the congregation, 'This is the word that the Lord has commanded to do.'
And Moses brought Aaron and his sons near and washed them with water.

And he put on him the tunic, and girded him with the sash, and clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod on him, and girded him with the woven waistband of the ephod, and fastened the ephod on him with it.
And he placed on him the breastpiece, and he put into the breastpiece the Urim and the Thummim.
And he placed the turban on his head, and on the turban, at the front of his face, he placed the gold plate, the holy diadem, as the Lord commanded Moses.
And Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and everything that was in it, and consecrated them.
And he sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times, and anointed the altar and all its utensils, and the basin and its stand, to consecrate them.
And he poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head and anointed him to consecrate him.
And Moses brought Aaron's sons near, and clothed them with tunics, and girded them with sashes, and tied headbands on them, as the Lord commanded Moses.
And he brought near the bull of the sin offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the bull of the sin offering.

And he killed it, and Moses took the blood and put it on the horns of the altar all around with his finger, and purified the altar from sin, and poured the blood at the base of the altar, and consecrated it to make atonement on it.
And he took all the fat that was on the entrails, and the lobe of the liver, and both kidneys and their fat, and Moses burned them as smoke on the altar.
And the bull and its hide and its flesh and its dung he burned in fire outside the camp, as the Lord commanded Moses.
And he brought near the ram for the burnt offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram.
And he killed it, and Moses sprinkled the blood on the altar all around.
And he cut the ram into its pieces, and Moses burned as smoke the head and the pieces and the fat.

And he washed the entrails and the legs with water, and Moses burned the entire ram as smoke on the altar—a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma, an offering by fire to the Lord—as the Lord commanded Moses.
And he brought near the second ram, the ram of ordination, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram.
And he killed it, and Moses took some of its blood and put it on Aaron's right earlobe, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.

And he brought Aaron's sons near, and Moses put some of the blood on their right earlobes, and on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet, and Moses sprinkled the blood on the altar all around.
And he took the fat—the fat tail, and all the fat that was on the entrails, and the lobe of the liver, and both kidneys and their fat—and the right thigh.

And from the basket of unleavened bread that was before the face of the Lord, he took one cake of unleavened bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, and placed them on the fat portions and on the right thigh.
And he put all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons, and waved them as a wave offering before the face of the Lord.
And Moses took them from off their palms and burned them as smoke on the altar on top of the burnt offering. These were ordination offerings for a pleasing aroma, an offering by fire to the Lord.
And Moses took the breast and waved it as a wave offering before the face of the Lord. From the ram of ordination, this was Moses' portion, according to the Lord's command to Moses.

And Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled it on Aaron, on his garments, and on his sons, and on the garments of his sons with him, and consecrated Aaron, his garments, and his sons, and the garments of his sons with him.

And Moses said to Aaron and to his sons, 'Boil the meat at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there you shall eat it and the bread that is in the basket of ordination offerings, as I commanded, saying: Aaron and his sons shall eat it.'
And what is left over of the meat and of the bread, you shall burn in fire.
And from the entrance of the tent of meeting, you shall not go out for seven days, until the day of your ordination is complete, for seven days will fill your hand.
As he has done on this day, the Lord commanded to do, to make atonement on your behalf.
And at the entrance of the tent of meeting you shall remain day and night for seven days, and you shall keep the charge of the Lord, and you shall not die, for thus I have been commanded.
And Aaron and his sons did all the things that the Lord commanded through the hand of Moses.
